LAW AND MOTION 302, CLAIMANT ANTONIO DAL BELLO'S MOTION TO RETURN PROPERTY ILLEGALLY SEIZED IS GRANTED IN PART. THE DIAMOND RING MUST BE RETURNED TO MR. DAL BELLO, WHILE THE CASH MAY BE RETAINED BY THE POLICE. THE PEOPLE HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THERE IS PROBABLE CAUSE TO BELIEVE THAT THE DIAMOND RING IS FORFEITABLE PROPERTY. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER FILED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Return Property Illegally Seized
Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rebecca Wagner, Assistant District Atttorney 415-553-4997 Office of the District Attorney for Petitioner People of the State of California.
Isaac Safier, Esq. 212-203-1750 of the Law Office of Rebecca Feigelson for Claimant Antonio Dalbello.

RULING - A R G U E D. Respondent Argues. Petitioner corrects language on the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CLAIMANT ANTONIO DALBELLO's Motion To Return Property Illegally Seized.

Claimant Antonio Dal Bello's motion to return property illegally seized is granted in part. The diamond ring must be returned to Mr. Dal Bello, while the cash may be retained by the police. The People have shown that there is probable cause to believe that the cash was furnished in exchange for controlled substances or intended to be used to facilitate a violation of the narcotics laws. The change in the law regarding marijuana since the seizure of the cash does NOT immunize the cash from forfeiture. Mr. Dal Ballo cites no authority, nor could the court locate any authority, holding that a change in law converts previously forfeitable property into non-forfeitable property. The People have not shown that there is probable cause to believe that the diamond ring is forfeitable property.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rebecca Wagner, Assistant District Attorney 415-553-4997 for Petitioner.
Isaac Safier, Esq. 212-203-1750 of the Law Office of Rebecca Feigelson for Claimants.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CLAIMANT ANTONIO DALBELLO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings to dismiss unverified petition for forfeiture in rem.

Claimant Antonio Dal Bello's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted with twenty court days leave to amend. The petition fails to allege sufficient ultimate facts to support forfeiture relief for any of the items of property that is sought to be forfeited. The People are given leave to remedy this deficiency if they can do so in good faith. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed in Open Court. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
